Interface Renderable2D

All Superinterfaces:
Renderable
All Known Subinterfaces:
PhysicalRenderable2D
All Known Implementing Classes:
ControlPanel, Sprite
public interface Renderable2D extends Renderable
A 2D renderable object is a 2-dimensional object with screen bounds, screen translation, rotation, and X/Y axis scaling. These operations are anchored at origin in the top-left corner.

Property methods (getters) must be side-effect-free, i.e. they should not do any modifications to the given rendering context or other stateful data structures to avoid unexpected side effects.

See Also:
  • Sprite - a general-purpose physical 2D renderable
  • Physical2D

    Method
    Description
    default double
    getAnchorX(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the X coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around.
    default double
    getAnchorY(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the Y coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around.
    Hitbox2D
    getBounds(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns this object's on-screen bounding hitbox, as if without any transformations applied.
    double
    getRotation(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the rotation in degrees for this object.
    double
    getScreenScaleX(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the X scale coefficient for this object.
    double
    getScreenScaleY(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the Y scale coefficient for this object.
    double
    getScreenX(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the X axis translation for this object.
    double
    getScreenY(RenderingContext ctx)
    Returns the Y axis translation for this object.
    void
    render(RenderCanvas canvas)
    Render this object onto the given canvas.

    Methods inherited from interface Renderable

    getGraphicEffects, getPreferredCanvasSize, isVisible

  • Method Details

    • render

      void render(RenderCanvas canvas)
      Render this object onto the given canvas. The canvas's size will not be more than values returned by Renderable.getPreferredCanvasSize(RenderingContext). The object must be rendered as if without any transformations applied.
      Specified by:
      render in interface Renderable
      Parameters:
      canvas - the canvas

    • getScreenX

      double getScreenX(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the X axis translation for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the X axis translation for this object

    • getScreenY

      double getScreenY(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the Y axis translation for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the Y axis translation for this object

    • getAnchorX

      default double getAnchorX(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the X coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around. Defaults to zero.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the X coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around

    • getAnchorY

      default double getAnchorY(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the Y coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around. Defaults to zero.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the Y coordinate of the point to rotate and scale around

    • getRotation

      double getRotation(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the rotation in degrees for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the rotation in degrees for this object

    • getScreenScaleX

      double getScreenScaleX(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the X scale coefficient for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the X scale coefficient for this object

    • getScreenScaleY

      double getScreenScaleY(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns the Y scale coefficient for this object.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the Y scale coefficient for this object

    • getBounds

      Hitbox2D getBounds(RenderingContext ctx)
      Returns this object's on-screen bounding hitbox, as if without any transformations applied. This can be used to check what object was clicked, what objects intersect, etc.

      Note: This method should not modify the given context in any way to avoid sudden side effects.

      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the bounding box
